## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey, it's Jeff Walker and today I wanted to talk to you about seed launches. So seed launches are those launches where you can start with no list, no product, and still do a really great product launch. Now this pine cone holds dozens of seeds from a ponderosa pine. Or dozens of seeds that are capable of creating a ponderosa pine. I mean, it's just absolutely amazing to think that just one little seed, and this one contains dozens or possibly even more than maybe a hundred seeds. ## [12:10 - 15:10]

whatever your subject area is, whatever your product area is, and you divide it into five main areas. So maybe, let's just pretend you're going to, your price is going to be about learning how to play guitar. So you split that into five main areas. And I'm sure whatever your topic is, whatever your area of expertise, you can split it into five main areas. So what are the five biggest topic areas of your subject? If you're going to, if you're going back to teaching guitar, I don't know, maybe the first one's learning how to tune the guitar, or maybe it's about learning how to hold the guitar, or maybe it's learning the different parts of the guitar. I don't know exactly how you would teach it. I am a beginning guitarist, but I'm probably not your best teacher. So let's just say you come up with your five main topics, and those will be your five calls. Before your first call, you send out an email saying something like, and we'll go back to the guitar example here, it'd be like, okay folks, this is the first, in this first call, I'm going to teach you how to tune the guitar and how to hold the guitar. But before we do that call, I need your help. Can you tell me what's your number one question, or you can say what are your top two questions you have about whatever the topic is, in this case, tuning the guitar and holding the guitar. Then you would send your people into the survey. So the people that signed up, you would send them into the survey. Again, there's great free tools you can use. The one I use is SurveyMonkey. I've been using it for more than a decade. There's lots of other alternatives you can use. You know, Google's got some way you can use their apps to set up surveys, or you can use one like SurveyGizmo. Like I said, the one I've been using for over a decade is SurveyMonkey.com. And I'm pretty sure there's a free version, there's a paid version. There's plenty of other tools out there. There's just a few. But the important thing is you run your people into the survey. In other words, you send them an email, send them to the survey, and they'll tell you what their top questions are. Then you simply just take those questions, and you group them into similar questions. You come up with a top 10, 12, 15 questions. And then on the day of your first call, you just call into your bridge line. All your attendees call into the bridge line as well. And you answer all those questions. You just run through those questions. It might take you 60 minutes. Maybe you go 90 minutes. If you want, you can open it up and take additional questions on the line. You're basically giving a presentation where you're answering all the top questions. It's that simple. If you're doing this in an area where you have some knowledge or expertise, it's really, really easy. And it's so much easier than to just do this, and to just try a lecture. Just to stand up and give a lecture, answering questions is, for most people, is much, much easier. So after that first call, then you go back, and you would survey your people again. You would say something like, well, that last call was great. I loved it. It was great interaction. It was so much fun teaching you guys. But I just want to make sure I covered everything we needed to about tuning the guitar.

## [15:10 - 18:11]

Do you have any additional questions that I didn't cover about tuning the guitar? What else can I help you with? And then you say, our next call is going to be whatever the next topic is. Maybe it's strumming the guitar. So our next call is going to be about a strumming the guitar. What are your top questions about strumming the guitar? And then you just do the same thing you did in the first call. You take all those questions, you group them up, you put them in order, and then you get on a call and you deliver it. This is how you create a great product, because it's so interactive. It's tuned to exactly what your clients want. Then you do one call a week, and five weeks later, you're done. You're done. Since all the calls are recorded, and that's generally done automatically with your bridge line, now you have the records, you've got a nearly complete product. If you did a webinar, then you just recorded your screen, and you've got five hours or more of video. You've got yourself a product.
